phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace, pull down the sample files, and initialize the azd project shape needed for the rest of the run.
phaseGoals.Provision: Provision the Azure-side infrastructure and verify that the expected account, project, model, and related resources exist.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package the sample, push the image if needed, and register or deploy the hosted agent so the remote endpoint exists.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remotely deployed sample the way a user or engineer would and verify the result, latency, and first-use behavior.
phaseGoals.Monitor: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a visible code change, redeploy it, and verify that the new behavior appears on a fresh remote execution path.
phaseGoals.Cleanup: Remove provisioned resources so the next run starts from a clean state and does not leak infrastructure.

executiveThemes.label: Harness or environment noise
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples were blocked by upstream harness state rather than by an independently exercised product failure. They should be read as run-noise and dependency fallout, not as clean sample verdicts.

executiveThemes.label: Postdeploy RBAC or identity failure
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples got through the core deploy path, but the postdeploy identity or RBAC steps did not finish, so the agent could not create sessions or complete remote work.

executiveThemes.label: Clean pass
executiveThemes.paragraph: This explorer entry was generated directly from Paperclip artifacts for HOL-4546. Samples marked clean pass completed all eight phases without recorded workaround use.

executiveThemes.label: Run integrity or infrastructure deletion
executiveThemes.paragraph: These failures are run-integrity problems. The underlying resource group or endpoint disappeared between phases, which makes downstream deploy, invoke, and monitor results unreliable as product signals.

executiveThemes.label: Needs review
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples need manual review before treating the explorer summary as final.

samples.firstFailure: Remote Invoke: Azure AI Foundry hosted agents platform returned HTTP 500 session_creation_failed on all 3 invoke attempts over ~5.5 minutes. Server-side issue — the endpoint accepts the request, assigns a session ID, then fails during session creation. Not an auth/RBAC issue (no 401/403). Deploy (P04) succeeded. Likely platform defect or transient outage in the northcentralus hosted agents backend.

P03 Local Run completed. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent run, azd ai agent invoke --local) failed due to missing azure.ai.agent service in azure.yaml — known template gap. Workaround: ran server directly via python main.py on port 8088, invoked via curl. Response verified: {"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}. No follow-up work identified.

P06 Monitor complete. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent show, azd ai agent monitor) failed with exit code 1: "no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml". Root cause: template structural bug — ai-foundry-starter-basic azure.yaml has no services section defining the agent. This is a template defect, not an execution or infrastructure failure. Structured artifact and log posted. No follow-up work identified.

Azure correlation IDs: None Status: ❌ FAILED — same root cause as show: missing azure.ai.agent service definition in azure.yaml Classification: Template structural bug

Root Cause

The ai-foundry-starter-basic template (used by echo-nonstreaming-py-invocations) azure.yaml contains:

name: ai-foundry-starter-basic
infra:
  provider: bicep
  path: ./infra
requiredVersions:
  extensions:
    azure.ai.agents: ">=0.1.0-preview"

No services section is defined. Both azd ai agent show and azd ai agent monitor require a service definition of type azure.ai.agent to resolve agent name/version. This is a template structural defect, not an execution or infrastructure failure.

Re-close: P03 was already fully executed and passed. Late JIT SSH token provisioning event caused spurious re-wake. No new work needed.

Re-close: Late JIT SSH token arrival

This issue was already fully executed and passed in the prior run (run cb8b790f). The wake reason jit_ssh_token_provisioned is a late-arriving harness event — the JIT token was provisioned after execution had already completed using credentials from an earlier issuance.

All substeps passed, final artifact posted. Re-closing.

P03 Local Run — PASS. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent run, azd ai agent invoke --local) succeeded. Multi-turn state persistence verified: Turn 2 correctly recalled name (Alice) and color (blue) from Turn 1. No follow-up work identified.

P03 Final Result — PASS

Multi-turn state persistence verified. Both canonical commands succeeded.

Summary

  • azd ai agent run: Server started on 8088, deps installed, ready in 2s ✅
  • azd ai agent invoke --local: Successfully invoked the agent ✅
  • Multi-turn test (curl with session_id): Turn 2 correctly recalled Alice + blue ✅

Responses

Turn 1: "Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is such a calming and classic color. 😊" Turn 2: "Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue. 😊"

Observations

  • PORT=8090 env var is ignored by azd ai agent run — always binds to 8088
  • azd ai agent invoke --local generates a new session ID each call, so curl with explicit agent_session_id is needed to properly test multi-turn persistence
  • Server has a 20s idle shutdown timeout

No follow-up work identified.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

azd ai agent invoke --local succeeded, so curl fallback was used only for the multi-turn session test (with explicit agent_session_id query param). Both paths work.

Curl was used for the authoritative multi-turn test because azd ai agent invoke --local generates its own session ID on each run, making it unsuitable for testing session continuity across two separate invocations. The curl path with explicit agent_session_id=hat-multiturn-test-001 properly tests the multi-turn state persistence.

Status: ✅ Not needed as fallback — both paths functional

    -d '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}'

Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is such a calming and classic color. 😊

azd ai agent invoke --local '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}'

Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is such a calming and versatile color—great choice! 😊

✅ azd ai agent invoke --local WORKS (it was failing earlier due to port conflict / server not running). Uses its own session ID, so not the same session as curl.

Note: Earlier 404 errors from azd ai agent invoke --local were caused by port conflicts and server restarts, not a product bug.

curl -N -X POST "http://localhost:8088/invocations?agent_session_id=hat-multiturn-test-001" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"message": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?"}'

Exit code: 0 Duration: 3s HTTP Status: 200

Response (SSE stream, full_text):

Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue. 😊

✅ Second response correctly recalls both name (Alice) and color (blue).

Recalls color (blue) ✅

P05 FAIL confirmed across 2 runs (3 attempts, ~7 min span). Persistent RBAC PermissionDenied — managed identity lacks Azure OpenAI data-plane permissions. SSH access works fine. No follow-up work identified.

Re-execution After JIT Token Refresh (Run 2)

Woke with jit_ssh_token_provisioned — fresh SSH credentials. Retried the first invoke.

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-21/multiturn-py-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}' 2>&1

Trace ID: a9b420b3-52ab-47e0-aed5-4ca731f63b3b Session: 73f82d6be5a01b4f00UQwIIYzZ09gGjwTSlRUmzVN9Jbb2Karm (turn 3)

Output:

Error calling Azure OpenAI: Error code: 401 - {"error": {"code": "PermissionDenied", "message": "Principal does not have access to API/Operation."}}

Result: FAIL — same RBAC PermissionDenied on third attempt. The managed identity still lacks the required Azure OpenAI data-plane permissions. This is a persistent RBAC role-assignment defect from the provision/deploy phase, not a transient propagation delay (original attempt was ~7 minutes ago).

SSH connectivity is fine — the issue is the deployed app's managed identity RBAC, not remote access.

Second invoke (multi-turn test) not attempted — first invoke never succeeded.

No follow-up work identified — result unchanged from Run 1.

P05 FAIL: RBAC auth issue — managed identity fc62abe6-4005-4f10-a58b-e7c75a83c23f lacks Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/OpenAI/responses/write. Initial attempt and 5-min retry both returned 401 PermissionDenied. Multi-turn state persistence test (second invoke) was not attempted. Log written to P05-remote-invoke.log. No follow-up work identified.

P05 Final Result

P07 complete. Deploy succeeded (v25). All invocations FAIL due to 401 PermissionDenied — postdeploy RBAC hook failed (AZURE_TENANT_ID not set). Applied 5-min RBAC retry rule, same result. Classification: auth issue / RBAC postdeploy defect. No follow-up work identified.

P07 Final Result: FAIL

Summary

  • Code change: ✅ PASS — appended UPDATED to system prompt
  • azd deploy: ✅ PASS — version 25 deployed (postdeploy hook failed with known AZURE_TENANT_ID issue)
  • New session invoke: ❌ FAIL — 401 PermissionDenied (both attempts, before and after 5-min RBAC retry)
  • Reused session invoke: ❌ FAIL — 401 PermissionDenied (no 500, same RBAC root cause)

Root Cause

The postdeploy hook that assigns RBAC roles failed because AZURE_TENANT_ID is not set in the execution environment. The managed identity principal fc62abe6-4005-4f10-a58b-e7c75a83c23f lacks Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/OpenAI/responses/write. This is the same auth defect cascading from earlier phases.

Classification: auth issue / RBAC postdeploy defect
